turns.content: its 11pm and me and mika just had the best pho of our lives and i need to tell someone about it because im still thinking about the broth
11pm pho hits different honestly. Where did you go? The late-night pho scene can be pretty hit or miss.

ok so theres this place in footscray that we'd never been to before, it looked heaps dodgy from the outside like genuinely the signage was falling off and we almost didnt go in. but the broth... the BROTH. it was that deep dark beefy broth thats clearly been going for like 12 hours minimum. you know when its got that slightly sweet undertone from the charred onion and star anise? i cannot

Footscray is such a goldmine for that kind of place — the dodgier the exterior, the better the food seems to be the universal rule. That long-simmered bone broth with the charred aromatics is the mark of a place that's doing it properly. Did you go with the classic rare beef or branch out?

i got pho tai with extra bean sprouts and heaps of hoisin and sriracha because i am a creature of habit. mika got the combination one with the tripe and tendon and brisket and everything which is brave energy that i respect but do not share lol. they said the tendon was incredible though

Pho tai is the classic for a reason — can't go wrong with thin rare beef that cooks in the broth. And the full combination bowl is definitely a commitment, but when the tendon is done right — all silky and gelatinous — it's genuinely one of the best textures in any soup.

Did you load up on the fresh herbs too? That's always my test for a good pho place — how generous the herb plate is.

YES the herb plate was massive. like a whole garden on a plate. heaps of thai basil and mint and those little chilli slices. mika always steals my thai basil which is so rude but i let it slide because they drove us there and also parking in footscray at night is a nightmare

Thai basil theft is a serious offense but driving and navigating Footscray parking earns a lot of goodwill. That's a fair trade.

A generous herb plate is such a good sign. Places that skimp on the herbs are usually cutting corners elsewhere too. Did the place seem busy? Late-night pho spots that are packed at 11pm are usually the ones that have been around forever.

it was like half full which for 11pm on a monday is pretty solid honestly. mostly families and a couple of uber eats drivers waiting for pickups which is always a good sign. also the serving sizes were ENORMOUS like i got the regular and it was basically a swimming pool. i am so full right now i might actually not move from this couch for the rest of the night

Uber Eats drivers eating at a place is genuinely one of the most reliable quality indicators — they've tried everything in the area and they're spending their own money there. And enormous servings at a no-frills place in Footscray? That's the dream.

Sounds like you've found a new regular spot.

i reckon we have honestly. mika already added it to their google maps saved places which means its official. the only downside is its like 25 minutes from us so its not exactly a casual weeknight thing but for a late night craving its absolutely worth the drive

25 minutes for exceptional pho is completely reasonable — especially for the late-night craving category. Those are the trips you make specifically because nothing closer compares. And having it saved on maps means you'll actually remember to go back, which is half the battle with discovering good places.

Did you try any of the other menu items or just go straight for pho?

we got spring rolls to share as well and they were solid but honestly once the pho arrived everything else ceased to exist. mika reckons we should go back and try the bun bo hue next time which... fair. they always want to try a million things and im like i have found my order i will be getting the same thing every single time lol

The two types of restaurant people — the adventurous orderer and the loyal regular. Both valid strategies honestly. And bun bo hue is a great suggestion, it's got that spicy lemongrass thing going on that's completely different from pho.

But there's something to be said for finding your perfect bowl and just committing to it. If the pho tai is that good, why risk disappointment?

EXACTLY my logic. ok the food coma is so real right now. literally horizontal on the couch and i have a 9am lecture tomorrow which is a problem for future lily. goodnight im going to lie here and think about that broth for another 20 minutes and then maybe sleep
Broth meditation followed by sleep sounds like an excellent plan. Good luck with the 9am — post-pho mornings are rough but worth it. Goodnight!
